summary: Introduces Graph Convolutional Networks (GCN), a semi-supervised learning method using a first-order spectral approximation for efficient graph convolution on node classification tasks.
Introduces GraphSAGE, an inductive node-embedding method using neighborhood sampling and aggregation to learn representations on large graphs without requiring the full graph at inference time.
Proposes ChebNet, a spectral graph convolution method using Chebyshev polynomial filters for fast localized filtering on graphs with improved computational efficiency.
Introduces Spectral Networks, a foundational framework for applying convolution to graphs through spectral graph theory and Fourier-domain methods.

Proposes the Message Passing Neural Network (MPNN) framework, a unified formulation for graph neural networks based on neural message passing for applications including graph classification and quantum chemistry.
Presents Adam, an adaptive learning rate method for stochastic optimization combining momentum and per-parameter adaptive rates.
Introduces Gated Graph Sequence Neural Networks (GGNN), a recurrent graph neural network approach using gated recurrent units for sequence modeling on graphs.
Proposes convolutional networks on graphs for learning molecular fingerprints, introducing aggregation-based convolution and pooling methods for non-Euclidean graph data.
